message:
feat(frankensqlite): upgrade to rev 9cedb30b, un-ignore all parity tests, reduce semantic search lock scope, add jittered retry backoff

Bump frankensqlite dependency from ff6a114b to 9cedb30b (~306 upstream
commits) which fixes ORDER BY, UPSERT, mixed aggregates, and OpenRead
bugs. This enables removing all #[ignore] annotations from the 16+
parity and concurrent stress tests that were previously blocked by
upstream limitations.

Semantic search refactored to release the monolithic mutex during
expensive I/O (embedding calls, ANN/two-tier index loads) by wrapping
index fields in Arc and extracting standalone methods
(semantic_query_embedding, ann_index, in_memory_two_tier_index,
progressive_context, search_semantic_candidates, etc.). QueryCache
split into get_cached + store to support this lock-release pattern.

All inline exponential backoff retry loops (8 call sites across
sqlite.rs, lib.rs, search/query.rs) replaced with a centralized
sleep_with_franken_retry_backoff function that adds randomized jitter
via splitmix64-style PRNG to prevent lock-step retry storms under
concurrent writer contention.

Additional fixes:
- SUM(0) workarounds reverted to plain 0 (mixed aggregates now correct)
- Unicode combining accent test expectation updated for NFC normalization
- AGENTS.md "Known Limitations" replaced with shorter "Known Differences"
- FTS duplicate-row test renamed and simplified (generation-key approach)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -261,12 +261,13 @@ Use `FrankenConnectionManager` for concurrent access:
 4. **Limit concurrent writers** to 4 threads (matches production rayon parallelism)
 5. **Retryable errors:** `Busy`, `BusyRecovery`, `BusySnapshot`, `WriteConflict`, `SerializationFailure`, `DatabaseCorrupt`
 
-### Known frankensqlite Limitations (during migration)
+### Known frankensqlite Differences
 
-- `ORDER BY` with `IS NULL` expressions not supported in SELECT list
-- Mixed aggregate and non-aggregate columns without GROUP BY not supported
-- `INSERT ON CONFLICT` (UPSERT) can fail after repeated calls in release mode
-- Some complex `SELECT` queries fail with "OpenRead" errors (result-loading path)
+- **File format incompatibility:** frankensqlite databases cannot be read by C
+  SQLite (rusqlite) and vice versa. Historical bundle salvage still uses rusqlite
+  for reading pre-migration databases.
+- **`PRAGMA writable_schema`:** Not supported for write operations (INSERT/UPDATE
+  on sqlite_master). SELECT from sqlite_master works.
 
 ### General Rules

@@ -32,7 +32,7 @@ which = "*"
 shell-words = "*"
 dotenvy = "*"
 notify = "*"
-frankensqlite = { git = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite", rev = "ff6a114b", package = "fsqlite", features = ["fts5"] }
+frankensqlite = { git = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite", rev = "9cedb30b", package = "fsqlite", features = ["fts5"] }
 rayon = "*"
 crossbeam-channel = "*"
 parking_lot = "*"
@@ -104,8 +104,8 @@ rand = "0.10"
 qrcode = { version = "*", optional = true }
 image = { version = "*", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["png"] }
 unicode-width = "0.2.2"
-# rusqlite used for FTS5 search fallback path: frankensqlite's in-memory FTS5
-# cannot read on-disk shadow tables, so we need stock SQLite for MATCH queries.
+# rusqlite retained for historical bundle salvage: reading pre-migration databases
+# created by C SQLite (frankensqlite uses a different on-disk format).
 rusqlite = { version = "*", features = ["bundled", "modern_sqlite"] }
 
 # macOS keychain access (optional, for ChatGPT decryption)
@@ -138,7 +138,7 @@ rand = "0.10"
 rand_chacha = "0.10"
 # frankensqlite compat gate tests (bead 3vvqa) — fsqlite-types needed for SqliteValue
 # enum variants in test assertions. The main fsqlite dep is available as `frankensqlite`.
-fsqlite-types = { git = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite", rev = "ff6a114b", package = "fsqlite-types" }
+fsqlite-types = { git = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite", rev = "9cedb30b", package = "fsqlite-types" }
 
 [[bin]]
 name = "cass"

@@ -47,7 +47,7 @@ const CONTRACTS: &[DependencyContract] = &[
         crate_package_name: "fsqlite",
         manifest_package_field: Some("fsqlite"),
         expected_git: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite",
-        expected_rev: "ff6a114b",
+        expected_rev: "9cedb30b",
         expected_version: "0.1.2",
         expected_features: &["fts5"],
         expected_default_features: None,
@@ -64,7 +64,7 @@ const CONTRACTS: &[DependencyContract] = &[
         crate_package_name: "fsqlite-types",
         manifest_package_field: Some("fsqlite-types"),
         expected_git: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite",
-        expected_rev: "ff6a114b",
+        expected_rev: "9cedb30b",
         expected_version: "0.1.2",
         expected_features: &[],
         expected_default_features: None,
